steps.title: Find the decisive evidence
steps.content: The passage says many North American studies found a positive association, and studies outside North America (including Luo’s in China) found the same thing, while few studies produced results like Porvari and Verta’s. The “suggesting that ______” blank must follow logically from this global pattern of agreement.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each choice against the evidence:
  • Choice A — ‘levels of dissolved organic carbon and mercury in bodies of fresh water are both much higher in Finland than elsewhere.’: the passage gives no data about absolute concentration levels in any country. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘the hypothesis that the positive association is particular to North America is correct.’: contradicted directly — Luo’s study in China showed the same association outside North America. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘dissolved organic carbon and mercury levels do typically rise and fall together in fresh water.’: supported by the worldwide pattern — most studies globally found the association, with rare exceptions. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘there were circumstances unique to Luo and colleagues’ study that impeded accurate measurements of mercury levels.’: the passage mentions nothing about measurement errors or unique circumstances in Luo’s study. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
